summary:
Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) plays a limited yet valuable role in preoperative locoregional staging of gastric cancer, serving as a feasible alternative to computed tomography when CT is contraindicated due to allergies or other concerns. It provides enhanced visualization of soft tissue structures, which supports critical assessments like detecting peritoneal spread—a factor vital in planning procedures such as metastasectomies. 

Note: This summary focuses only on direct clinical relevance related to staging accuracy, safety considerations, and applicability within current guidelines.
